title: Homerland

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase that signals we're in for Homer's bumbling
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpson opener - economical and character-perfect but not original by this point in the series

jokes.text: I grew up in a little town in Arkansas, whose name, ironically, was Terrorism.
jokes.setup: Serious documentary-style narrator discussing terrorism threats
jokes.punchline: The complete absurdity of a town actually being named 'Terrorism'
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of serious documentary tone with absurd premise - well-crafted misdirection

jokes.text: Ooh, there's new marshmallows in the Belfast Charms!
jokes.punchline: Pun on Lucky Charms using Belfast (referencing The Troubles)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing cereal marketing with political conflict - vintage Simpsons edge

jokes.text: No! No, that's Bart's cereal. It's the only way I can get him to take his 'vitamins.'
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals she's drugging Bart's breakfast
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's methods, air quotes add timing

jokes.text: The sum of the square roots of any two sides of an isosceles triangle is equal to the square root of the remaining side. That's not right. Yes, it is. They're my lines as the Scarecrow in The Wizard of Oz.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's quoting the famously wrong Scarecrow line
jokes.explanation: Excellent reference joke - meta commentary on famous movie error, perfect for Homer's character

jokes.text: It's a little sad-- the guys who did the funny skit last year, well, they're all very sick.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that nuclear workers are dying from radiation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Homer's characteristic obliviousness - shows the show's satirical edge

jokes.text: Even better. I never unpacked from the time we went to Hawaii.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if he packed everything
jokes.punchline: Homer's been living out of a suitcase for an extended period
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - laziness disguised as efficiency

jokes.text: Whoo! My lava's almost cooled!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably showing off Hawaiian souvenirs
jokes.punchline: He's apparently brought back actual lava
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual gag - Homer would definitely try to bring home lava as a souvenir

jokes.text: I can't believe I have to miss taking the kids to buy gym uniforms. Send me a picture?
jokes.setup: Marge being overly concerned about missing mundane activities
jokes.punchline: Wanting photographic evidence of this incredibly boring errand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge characterization - finding deep meaning in mundane parental duties

jokes.text: Poor guy. Salt of the earth.
jokes.punchline: Marge's completely sincere assessment of Homer as virtuous
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge delusion about Homer's character - reliable character comedy

jokes.text: Man I love conventions. Yeah, they're the perfect combination of work and binge drinking.
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of conventions as drinking excuses
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor about convention culture

jokes.text: Now look guys, we are here for one important reason. To get free swag. Swag, I'm gonna grab forever. Somethin' I really don't need. Swag! I'm gonna have 40 key chains. And I don't even have 40 keys. Swag!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining their mission at the convention
jokes.punchline: Escalating obsession with useless free items, punctuated by repeated 'Swag!'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Homer's enthusiastic irrationality about free stuff

jokes.text: ♪ More free stuff ♪ ♪ More free stuff ♪ Uh, all I have are these spec sheets. ♪ More free stuff, more free stuff ♪ ♪ Swag!
jokes.punchline: Booth worker's mundane offering contrasted with Homer's musical enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation with nice contrast between Homer's excitement and reality

jokes.text: There's that woman I always have a 'same time next year' affair with.
jokes.setup: Lenny spotting a familiar face at the convention
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of annual adultery
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually - good reference to the movie while revealing character details

jokes.text: And she's with her frumpy friend I always have dinner with when you two disappear. Mm-hmm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Carl responding to Lenny's affair comment
jokes.punchline: Carl's resigned acceptance of being the awkward third wheel
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Carl's sad acceptance of his role

jokes.text: Put up your nukes! Halpern! Hey-hey! The wild man of Wichita! I still haven't forgiven you for putting that dead goat in my bed. He wasn't dead till you rolled over on him! Ha!
jokes.punchline: Escalating from goat prank to accidental goat murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from prank to dark absurdity - unexpected and memorable

jokes.text: Halpern, what will you think of next? Nothing. This is it.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about Halpern's next innovation
jokes.punchline: Halpern's blunt admission he's peaked
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery - unexpected honesty about creative limitations

jokes.text: Hey, here's a brainstorm for you. Check if it's plugged in before you call I.T.! You think I like talking you monkeys through a password change?!
jokes.punchline: Breaking into frustrated rant about user incompetence
jokes.explanation: Relatable IT frustration - good character moment for background character

jokes.text: Homer, Homer-- when did I see him last? Geez, I don't know. Yeah, Homer's a great guy, but that doesn't mean I constantly think about whether he's still alive or not.
jokes.punchline: Lenny's casual attitude about his friend's mortality
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing realistic friendship boundaries

jokes.text: You're taking a picture of 'em in their moment of grief? It's okay. This camera has an 'auto cheer' feature.
jokes.punchline: Absurd technological solution to emotional inappropriateness
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of technology solving social problems poorly

jokes.text: Maybe he was mistaken for a dead elephant and flown back to Kenya. You're talking about my husband. To spare your feelings, we'll just call him the Blob.
jokes.punchline: His 'sensitive' alternative is equally insulting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - trying to be tactful while being worse

jokes.text: Two to one says the Blob is stuck in the water-intake pipe at the reservoir.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum taking bets on Homer's demise
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the Wiggum insensitivity running gag

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late, everyone. I had some trouble getting the voice mails off the 9-1-1 line. Why does everyone have to talk so fast and panicky? A man with a gun is in my house. Gibberish.
jokes.punchline: Dismissing genuine emergency calls as 'gibberish'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Wiggum incompetence - perfectly captures his oblivious negligence

jokes.text: Well, all the pay phones at the airport were replaced by self-serve yogurt. I ate all the cookie dough toppings a man could want. So many cookies will never be born.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he couldn't call
jokes.punchline: Yogurt topping consumption preventing cookie births
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd Homer logic with poetic ending about cookie genocide

jokes.text: Dear Christian God... Hey! Sorry. Dear God-- you know which one I mean-- thank you for returning our Homie.
jokes.punchline: Self-correcting to be religiously inclusive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's considerate nature even in prayer

jokes.text: It's hard to sleep with one unkissed cheek. Yeah, it's tough being man of the house. You left some big underpants to fill.
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal interpretation of 'big shoes to fill'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that's character-appropriate for Bart's crude humor

jokes.text: I didn't know they made Underoos in size 52. They're called Superoos, son. With pictures of the cast of The Expendables. More like The Expandables.
jokes.punchline: Homer's dad joke about action movie title
jokes.explanation: Standard dad joke - functional but predictable pun

jokes.text: Why didn't you strangle me? That kind of small-scale violence solves nothing.
jokes.punchline: Homer's philosophical rejection of his signature move
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's change is immediately apparent through violence philosophy

jokes.text: Ta-da! Pork chops crusted with Cheeto dust.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting fusion of junk food and main course
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor typical of Simpson family dining

jokes.text: I'll just enjoy these green beans with slivered almonds. Mmm, so slivered. Mmm.
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic appreciation of 'slivered' quality
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's awkward attempt at sophistication

jokes.text: Bart, why is the dad I always wished for creeping me out?
jokes.punchline: Getting what she wanted but finding it disturbing
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Lisa's discomfort with positive change is very human

jokes.text: I don't know. 'Cause you're incapable of experiencing joy? Yay. Point taken.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why she's creeped out
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal psychological assessment and Lisa's deadpan acceptance
jokes.explanation: Great sibling dynamic - Bart's insight and Lisa's self-awareness

jokes.text: Water? That stuff killed my grandmother. So sad.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for water at Moe's
jokes.punchline: Barney's illogical fear of water
jokes.explanation: Good Barney character moment - alcoholic logic about dangerous water

jokes.text: I'm not sure a man who eats right and doesn't drink can be good in bed.
jokes.punchline: Linking sobriety to sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Adult humor showing Marge's practical concerns about Homer's transformation

jokes.text: You're so beautiful when I cut you off in the middle of a question.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his interruption
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor - Homer acknowledging his rudeness while being charming about it

jokes.text: What's that thing you're doing? Moving my body.
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact description of basic movement
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - Homer's sedentary lifestyle makes movement noteworthy

jokes.text: This is worse than when he went to New Orleans and came back with a Southern accent. Uh, how y'all doin'?
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing current situation to past changes
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible Southern accent impression
jokes.explanation: Standard callback structure showing Homer's susceptibility to influence

jokes.text: Chief Wiggum, how come every time there's a terrorist chatter in this town, you come to me? Lay off, Apu. When I look at people, I don't see colors. I just see crackpot religions.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's 'progressive' stance is actually religious profiling
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of prejudice disguised as tolerance - sharp social commentary

jokes.text: Yello? Is this line secure? Uh, it's a little jiggly but it'll hold.
jokes.setup: Mysterious caller asking about secure line
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation about physical stability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misunderstanding - literal interpretation of security terminology

jokes.text: We got intel-- uh, that's short for a word I don't know... Intelligence? I don't believe so, no.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to sound official with 'intel'
jokes.punchline: Self-aware rejection of intelligence connection
jokes.explanation: Great Wiggum self-awareness about his own incompetence

jokes.text: Dad's kneeling on a prayer mat. He doesn't believe in kneeling.
jokes.punchline: Homer's philosophical opposition to kneeling
jokes.explanation: Good character observation - Homer's laziness extends to religious practices

jokes.text: If you are a cat, prove it. Do you hate Mondays, like Garfield? And do you love lasagna, like me? Meow. Okay then.
jokes.punchline: Using Garfield comics as cat authentication, accepting meow as proof
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Wiggum's security methods are completely nonsensical

jokes.text: He's like a husband in a widow's memory-- perfect. Perfect.
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to idealized memory of dead spouse
jokes.explanation: Dark, poetic humor with excellent economy - captures the unsettling nature of Homer's change

jokes.text: No. We're not even here right now.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if they're talking about him
jokes.punchline: Agent's nonsensical denial of their presence
jokes.explanation: Standard spy comedy trope - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: And don't believe what you've heard about me. I haven't heard anything about you except from you.
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out he's creating his own bad reputation
jokes.explanation: Good logical observation - Lisa's intelligence catching the agent's mistake

jokes.text: Lisa, I'll miss you when this is all over. When what's all over? This conversation. See you on the other side. What other side? Of the house. Where the fireplace is. We're toasting marshmallows. Marsh... mallows.
jokes.punchline: Escalating deflations to mundane marshmallow activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure - builds tension then deflates to domestic normalcy

jokes.text: I'm Bart Simpson. Who the hell are you? Agent Crawford, FBI. You guys know I don't talk to field agents. Get your boss on the phone.
jokes.punchline: 10-year-old demanding to speak to FBI management
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - treating FBI like playground hierarchy

jokes.text: Go back to sleep, Milhouse. How do you know my name? I didn't. But I do now.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally getting intelligence through Milhouse's response
jokes.explanation: Good spy comedy moment - accidental intelligence gathering

jokes.text: I can torture you. I can give you incredible sex. Or you can just tell me what I want to know. What was the first one again?
jokes.punchline: Homer's interest in torture over sex
jokes.explanation: Character humor showing Homer's priorities - pain over pleasure

jokes.text: You think I'm beautiful? Oh! In a breaking-and-entering kind of way.
jokes.punchline: Agent clarifying it's criminal-context beauty
jokes.explanation: Decent clarification humor but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: And if you knew me, you'd know just how odd that sounds.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he needs to get to work on time
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness about his work ethic
jokes.explanation: Good character self-awareness about his laziness

jokes.text: You're in too deep, Annie. Get out. Are you real, or my imagination? Either way, my advice is sound.
jokes.punchline: Doesn't matter if voice is real, advice remains valid
jokes.explanation: Meta comedy about the nature of internal voices and advice.

jokes.text: You laugh now, but you won't be laughing soon. He's right, because now I go back to remembering that my cat just died.
jokes.punchline: Guard's unrelated personal tragedy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - threat becomes personal grief

jokes.text: My terrorist masters are always talking about you, Lisa.
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about terrorists discussing Lisa
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic about terrorist priorities

jokes.text: They wanted gas, grass or ass, and, brother, I had the ass.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how he paid for the ride
jokes.punchline: Homer offering his posterior as payment
jokes.explanation: Crude humor playing on old saying - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Pigs aren't happy to be made into pork chops. This T-shirt lied to me.
jokes.punchline: Blaming a t-shirt for misinformation about pig happiness
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - Homer trusting apparel for animal psychology

jokes.text: Oh, I've been listening to this song for three days, and it's only the end of the first verse!
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustration with jam band song length
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about jam band culture

jokes.text: Lisa, I've never prayed to a city in my life, and if I did, it would be Hershey, Pennsylvania.
jokes.setup: Lisa accusing him of praying to Mecca
jokes.punchline: Homer's chocolate-based theological preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor - perfect Homer priorities mixing religion with candy

jokes.text: Aw, sweetie, you told me a thousand things a million times. Even with a brain the size of a dinosaur's, I couldn't take that in.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying she's told him about pollution before
jokes.punchline: Homer's math about information overload and dinosaur brain comparison
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Homer's cognitive limitations

jokes.text: Just like when I smell up the toilet.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his harmless sabotage plan
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Basic toilet humor - in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Lou, Lou, Lou. Lou-Lou-Lou-Lou-Lou...! I can complete my dad's mission just like George W. Bush.
jokes.punchline: Lisa comparing herself to Bush completing his father's work
jokes.explanation: Political humor about presidential legacies - moderately clever

jokes.text: There's some things they just don't teach you in the police academy... movies.
jokes.punchline: Revealing his training source was the movie franchise
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about police competence and movie references

jokes.text: No, I was just up late watching a movie. Something with William Holden and...
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if he was shot
jokes.punchline: Homer's mundane explanation for tiredness
jokes.explanation: Basic misdirection - functional but not memorable

jokes.text: Wait a minute, Burns. You don't have a functioning AC system at a nuclear plant? That's against the law. That's impossible.
jokes.setup: Burns revealing the plant has no AC
jokes.punchline: FBI more concerned with building code than nuclear safety
jokes.explanation: Good irony about bureaucratic priorities

jokes.text: Sir, that's two years from now, and you're not supposed to tell anyone. Oopsie.
jokes.setup: Burns mentioning moving plant to China
jokes.punchline: Smithers revealing it's a future spoiler, Burns' casual response
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about plot spoilers and Burns' carelessness

jokes.text: Prisons are still made of mud and wattles, right? No. Concrete.
jokes.punchline: His outdated knowledge of prison construction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns characterization - his extreme age showing

jokes.text: A beer from the sky! It's a sign!
jokes.punchline: Interpreting falling beer as divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - finding cosmic meaning in alcohol

jokes.text: I wish a mission could bring me closer to someone, but it can't.
jokes.setup: FBI agent observing Homer and Lisa's bond
jokes.punchline: Agent's lonely confession about her work
jokes.explanation: Standard character moment - functional but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Amazingly, the hamster's older than I am.
jokes.punchline: Impossible age comparison with short-lived animal
jokes.explanation: Absurdist throwaway line - decent but not exceptional

tmdb_overview: A parody of "Homeland". Homer starts to act strangely polite after returning from a nuclear power plant convention, prompting a suspicious Lisa to ask an intense FBI agent named Annie (Kristen Wiig) to help her solve this mystery.
